pr.title: INFERENG-6968: KServe P/D entrypoint script only considers first NIC / HCA
pr.body:
The RoCE entrypoint script in P/D config templates referenced the ucx_hcas
bash array variable without an index or splat (${ucx_hcas}), which only
expands to the first element. This caused NVSHMEM_HCA_LIST and UCX_NET_DEVICES
to contain only one HCA instead of all detected HCAs. Use the same
$(IFS=,; echo "${ucx_hcas[*]}") pattern already used for NCCL_IB_HCA to
produce the full comma-separated list.

Closes [INFERENG-6968](https://redhat.atlassian.net/browse/INFERENG-6968)

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
Signed-off-by: David Whyte-Gray <dagray@redhat.com>

<!-- This is an auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->
## Summary by CodeRabbit

* **Bug Fixes**
  * Ensure RoCE-related environment variables (NVSHMEM_HCA_LIST, UCX_NET_DEVICES) are derived as comma-separated lists from detected HCAs when auto-inferred, so inferred network device values are consistently formatted across templates, startup scripts, and install manifests.
<!-- end of auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->
